mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 365369M as of 2006-01-01 reflects robust consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ months, monetary tightening constrains credit availability and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.29% as of 2006-01-01 approaching restrictive threshold
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5% for 2+ months, SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of 2006-01-01 near cyclical lows
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) and payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) jointly signal robust productive capacity utilization and labor demand. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days are positive due to earnings expansion expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.269 and PAYEMS at 134564K as of 2006-01-01
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized supply and labor growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (INDPRO × PAYEMS growth) falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint growth periods produce positive 20d returns in < 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling a contraction in residential investment and forward-looking weakness in construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1933K as of 2006-12-01, elevated but vulnerable to rate sensitivity
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market vulnerability
failure_condition: When HOUST falls > 10% MoM, SPX produces negative 5d returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, creating a negative real rate environment that initially supports equity valuations through asset price inflation, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days until policy response occurs.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 197.7 and FEDFUNDS at 4.29% as of 2006-01-01
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month, signaling labor market softening and reduced consumer income growth, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134564K as of 2006-01-01 with strong recent growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deceleration
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth < 100K, SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) both accelerate simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand expansion and earnings visibility. This joint signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth and profit expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11233.5B and RSAFS at 365369M as of 2006-01-01
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (GDPC1 growth × RSAFS growth) falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint acceleration periods produce positive 20d returns in < 58% of instances.
